title: News — SARAH CHAMPION MP
text_excerpt: News — SARAH CHAMPION MP News About Sarah Rotherham Contact Gallery Back News News Archive Back Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Back Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts News News News Archive About Sarah Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Rotherham Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts Contact Gallery Sarah Champion MP Sponsors “Gold Standard Bill” to Stop Fox Hunting Sarah Champion MP is one of 12 co-sponsors of the Hunting Act 2004 (Amendment) Bill, which has been presented to Parliament. Fox hunting has continued despite the 2004 Hunting Act under the guise of “trail hunting” - a smokescreen used to hunt foxes with hounds. Read More Eleanor Mclynn April 27, 2026 MPs strengthen law on hate crimes following campaign backed by Sarah Champion The MP for Rotherham who backed a campaign for a new law to equalise hate crime laws for LGBT+ and disabled people has expressed her delight that the change she campaigned on for a decade has now been passed by MPs and will become law. She also welcomed the Government’s move to introduce a new crime of hate motivated by misogyny for the first time. Read More Eleanor Mclynn April 17, 2026 Sarah Champion welcomes funding boost for Hopian in Rotherham to strengthen community cohesion - Hopian in Rotherham awarded government funding to help bring people together and bridge divides - Part of over £2 million investment supporting more than 200 grassroots organisations across England - Funding will help strengthen unity, trust and opportunity within local communities Sarah Champion, Member of Parliament for Rotherham has welcomed new government funding awarded to Hopian recognising its vital work in bringing people together and strengthening community ties locally. Read More Eleanor Mclynn April 17, 2026 Labour delivers for next generation defence workers with £50m for Defence Technical Excellence Colleges, including at Rotherham College The Labour Government has announced 5 Defence Technical Excellence Colleges (DTECs) across England, supported by £50m in funding. This is a commitment that the Government made in the Defence Industrial Strategy, and is now delivering on. DTECs will be existing further education colleges, recognised for providing ‘technical excellence’ and high-quality defence-focused education. They will provide training and education in crucial skills required by the defence sector, for students aged 16+, giving young people and adults the opportunity to gain the skills necessary to work well-paid, highly-skilled jobs and careers in the defence sector. Read More Alexander Guest April 14, 2026 Sarah Champion MP Responds to Launch of Orgreave Inquiry “I'm delighted work will soon begin on the Orgreave Inquiry. This fulfils a key promise made to my constituents and will hopefully be the fulfilment of the long struggle for justice of former mineworkers and the Orgreave Truth and Justice Campaign.’ Read More Alexander Guest March 26, 2026 New Best Start nursery comes to Rotherham · Major expansion of school-based nurseries to deliver cost of living support worth up to £8,000 a year, with more nursery places in Rotherham from September to help parents balance work and family life · Comes as Labour’s early years policies half the cost of childcare, saving parents £8,000 on average and bringing costs back to 2005 levels · Families in Rotherham to feel the benefit of accessible and affordable childcare, as part of Labour’s plan to cut the cost of living Read More Alexander Guest March 23, 2026 Older Powered by Squarespace
